meaning: n.消沉,衰退,(物价)暴跌v.失败,消沉,(物价)暴跌,跌落
examples: The toy industry is in a slump.
The likelihood is that the slump will be followed by a period of buoyancy.
The slump on Wall Street set up a chain reaction in stock markets around the world.
May's election saw Plaid slump to 11 seats - its worst tally since the assembly was established.
This is the biggest slump for any French president in the past 50 years.
